title: Bitcoin and Anarchism
description: Bitcoin governance is not a voting democracy; validity is enforced by voluntary rule-following nodes rather than ballots. This aligns with anarchist and cypherpunk ideas about non-coercive coordination.

resources.title: The Crypto Anarchist Manifesto
resources.notes: Foundational cypherpunk framing for non-state digital coordination.

resources.title: BIP 148: UASF SegWit Activation
resources.notes: Practical case where rule enforcement came from node operators.

resources.title: Cypherpunk Movement
resources.notes: Context for the political philosophy around Bitcoin's origins.
